project.name: Cyborg
project.description: Autonomous AI agent that communicates and acts across WhatsApp, email, voice chat, and phone calls. It maintains its own memory, manages contacts and calendars, and can reach out to people on your behalf through any supported channel.

nodes.name: llm-chart.tsx
nodes.summary: Stacked bar chart component for visualizing LLM call categories over 24-hour time buckets using Recharts. Renders color-coded bars for WhatsApp, email, voice, and other call categories.

nodes.name: categoryColor
nodes.summary: Maps a call category string to its display color, defaulting to gray.

nodes.name: categoryLabel
nodes.summary: Formats a category key into a human-readable label by replacing underscores and capitalizing words.

nodes.name: LLMChart
nodes.summary: React component rendering a responsive stacked bar chart of LLM call categories. Uses ResizeObserver for dynamic width adaptation.

nodes.name: session-list.tsx
nodes.summary: Displays a compact list of active sessions with relative timestamps, call counts, and navigation links to session detail views.

nodes.name: RelativeTime
nodes.summary: Renders a relative time string (e.g. '5m ago', '2h ago') from an ISO timestamp.

nodes.name: SessionList
nodes.summary: Renders a list of session items with channel indicators, call counts, and relative activity timestamps.

nodes.name: summary-cards.tsx
nodes.summary: Card-based display for recent session summaries showing topics with rich-text rendering, participant info, and relative timestamps.

nodes.name: relativeTime
nodes.summary: Converts an ISO timestamp to a human-readable relative time string.

nodes.name: shortKey
nodes.summary: Shortens a session key by extracting the last two colon-separated segments.

nodes.name: SummaryCards
nodes.summary: Renders summary items as cards with session keys, topics using RichText, and relative timestamps.

nodes.name: rich-text.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders text with inline contact references ({{contact:id|name}}) as clickable links to the contact detail page. Shared utility component used across multiple views.

nodes.name: RichText
nodes.summary: Parses text for {{contact:id|name}} patterns and renders them as navigable contact links while displaying plain text for the rest.

nodes.name: use-live-data.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hooks for real-time data: useWSEvents accumulates recent WebSocket events, and useWSConnected polls the WebSocket connection state via useSyncExternalStore.

nodes.name: useWSEvents
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to WebSocket events and maintains a rolling buffer of the last 100 events using useSyncExternalStore.

nodes.name: useWSConnected
nodes.summary: React hook that returns the current WebSocket connection status, polled every second via useSyncExternalStore.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: Core HTTP client providing fetchAPI, postAPI, and putAPI functions with cookie-based dashboard secret authentication. All route pages depend on this module for backend communication.

nodes.name: getSecret
nodes.summary: Extracts the dashboard secret from the cyborg_dashboard_secret cookie for API authentication.

nodes.name: fetchAPI
nodes.summary: Performs an authenticated GET request to the dashboard API, appending the secret as a query parameter.

nodes.name: postAPI
nodes.summary: Performs an authenticated POST request with a JSON body to the dashboard API.

nodes.name: putAPI
nodes.summary: Performs an authenticated PUT request with a JSON body to the dashboard API.

nodes.name: ws-client.ts
nodes.summary: WebSocket client singleton (DashboardWS) with automatic reconnection, exponential backoff, event listener management, and cookie-based authentication. Exported as a singleton instance used throughout the app.

nodes.name: DashboardWS
nodes.summary: WebSocket client class managing persistent connections with auto-reconnect, exponential backoff (1s to 30s), listener pub/sub, and secret-based authentication.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that initializes React with TanStack Router, TanStack Query, and StrictMode. Configures the router with the generated route tree and QueryClient with cache-friendly defaults.

nodes.name: routeTree.gen.ts
nodes.summary: Auto-generated route tree by TanStack Router that wires all route modules into a hierarchical routing structure. Imports every route file and exports the combined routeTree.

properties.description: Comprehensive documentation for the Memory Wiki System - a persistent, structured knowledge base that survives across conversations. Covers architecture, file-based storage, authoring, dream process, retrieval, access control, CLI commands, logging, and dashboard integration.

Describes the full pipeline: prompt assembly integrates memory index, tools (memory_write/read/search/browse) operate on the filesystem, heartbeat triggers reflection, dream process curates bulletins into entries.
All memory data lives under <workspace_dir>/memory/ as markdown files. Covers directory structure, access.yml configuration, and per-wiki indexes (_index.md).
Two paths for creating memory: real-time via memory_write tool during conversations, and post-session via heartbeat reflection that generates memory_prompts from session summaries.
Curation pipeline that transforms raw bulletins into structured, categorized memory entries using LLM-driven CREATE/UPDATE/IGNORE operations.
Three retrieval mechanisms: lightweight index always in prompt, memory_read for single entries, memory_search for LLM-powered semantic search, and memory_browse for category listing.
Wiki-level read/write access policies: always, trusted, or never. Trust checked against session_participants table.
Bulk-processes historical session summaries to populate the memory wiki retroactively.
Every memory_search call logged to memory_search_log table with query, results, session, latency.
Every dream run logged to memory_dream_log table with bulletins processed, entries created, raw LLM response.
Dashboard at /memory with stats, live search, pending bulletins, dream log, lint, and content viewer. Lists all API endpoints.

Reference table of source files: memory_service.py, memory_tools.py, prompt_assembler.py, workspace_tools.py, session_summary_service.py, heartbeat.py, cli.py, schemas, dashboard_api.py, UI component.

Documentation for the Outreach system - a mechanism for the Cyborg AI agent to proactively initiate WhatsApp conversations with trusted contacts, pursue a defined objective, and relay results back. Covers architecture, flow, trust model, data model, tools, dispatch lifecycle, and system prompt assembly.
Outreach enables proactive WhatsApp conversations on behalf of users. Multi-session coordination spanning two independent WhatsApp DM sessions.
Architecture diagram showing requestor session, target session, and WhatsApp bridge. Agent uses different tool sets in each session.
Three-phase flow: Initiation (send_whatsapp_to_contact), Target Conversation (bridge detects outreach, injects tools), Completion (finish_outreach relays result).

Reference table of key source files: whatsapp_outreach_tools.py, whatsapp_bridge_service.py, tool_registry.py, session_route_service.py, session_agenda_service.py, session_dispatch_gate.py, prompt_assembler.py, tap.py, llm_dispatch.py.
Three trust tiers: Unverified (no outreach), Known Untrusted (restricted), Trusted (full capabilities including outreach). SessionAgendaService selects system prompt by tier.
Session key format: agent:{agent_id}:whatsapp:{kind}:{identifier}. Phone-to-JID and JID-to-phone conversion rules.
Outreach state machine lives in session_routes.metadata JSON column. No separate outreach table.
Tools available in each session phase: requestor session, target session, result dispatch.
Tool assembly flow: build_common_tools() from tool_registry.py, then channel-specific tools from bridge service. Outreach metadata detection adds finish_outreach.
LLM dispatch flow: asyncio.Task, session lock, mark dispatched, chat_with_tools, tap fallback for missing send tool, NO_REPLY handling.
System prompt layers: workspace prompt, trust-tier agenda, participants, outreach prompt, trusted memory index.
Outreach actions logged in unified LLM call log under call_category whatsapp_outreach in both source and target sessions.

Documentation for the Skills System - a plugin mechanism for the Cyborg LLM agent to perform structured multi-step actions. Covers file structure, skill format, architecture (discovery, index injection, on-demand activation), component reference, caching, dashboard integration, and creating new skills.
Skills are a plugin mechanism for structured multi-step actions. Design goals: zero-code registration, lazy loading, hot reload, sandboxed execution.
Skills live under <workspace>/skills/ as directories. Required: skill.md. Optional: .py scripts, pyproject.toml, README.md.
YAML-like frontmatter (name, description, trigger) plus markdown body with step-by-step instructions for the LLM.
Three-stage pipeline: discover (scan skills/), index (build compact string for system prompt), activate (on-demand load full instructions). Includes detailed flow diagrams.
skill_loader._scan_skills() iterates skills/, caches by mtime hash, builds compact index, injects into system prompt via prompt_assembler.
LLM calls use_skill() -> skill_loader.load_skill() -> returns full instructions -> LLM calls run_script() via uv run in sandboxed environment.
Detailed reference for skill_loader.py, workspace_tools.py, skill_env.py, prompt_assembler.py, dashboard_api.py with function descriptions.
Module-level caches keyed by mtime hash. Automatic invalidation on skill add/edit/delete. No server restart needed.
GET /api/skills/installed endpoint lists all skills with metadata for the dashboard UI.
Step-by-step guide to creating skills with minimal and script-including examples.

nodes.name: 285_harness_logs.sql

layers.name: Server Core
layers.description: Central Python application layer containing FastAPI app bootstrap, Pydantic models, database setup, configuration, CLI commands, dependency injection, and the main orchestration logic that ties all subsystems together.

layers.name: Business Logic Services
layers.description: Domain-specific service modules handling LLM dispatch, session management, email polling/delivery, voice processing, memory, contact management, tool registry, skill delegation, subagent orchestration, WhatsApp outreach, and prompt assembly.

layers.name: API Routes
layers.description: FastAPI router modules exposing REST and WebSocket endpoints for contacts, calendars, email, phone calls, sessions, and the dashboard (API and live WebSocket event stream).

layers.name: WhatsApp Bridge
layers.description: Go microservice that interfaces with WhatsApp via the whatsmeow library, providing persistent message queuing, WebSocket protocol handling, and event relay to the Python server.

layers.name: Dashboard UI
layers.description: React TypeScript single-page application built with TanStack Router and Vite, providing the operator dashboard with session views, contact management, phone call inspection, workspace tools, skills monitoring, and real-time WebSocket updates.

layers.name: Database Schema
layers.description: SQLite schema migration files and table definitions covering sessions, contacts, tasks, projects, notifications, calendars, email relay, phone calls, LLM logging, evaluations, memory, and all other persistent data structures.

layers.name: Evaluation Framework
layers.description: LLM-based evaluation system with test cases for tool calling, email handling, voice chat, skill delegation, workspace tools, and dream processing, including a judge mechanism and case registry.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Markdown documentation files including architecture guides for memory, outreach, and skills, design specifications, and their structured sections.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Python test suites covering API endpoints, CLI commands, configuration, context building, project autonomy and execution, prompt history, webhook handling, and acceptance tests for the gateway and reasoning subsystems.

layers.name: Voice Assets
layers.description: Static assets for voice interactions: language lesson content, system prompt templates, reference voice audio samples, and the standalone voice frontend SPA with WebRTC support.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Build and package configuration files including pyproject.toml, package.json, tsconfig, Go module files, and Makefiles.

tour.title: Welcome to Cyborg
tour.description: Start with the project README to understand Cyborg's purpose as an autonomous AI agent, then look at the main entry point where the FastAPI application is assembled.

tour.title: Foundation Layer: Database, Models, and Context
tour.description: Explore the core infrastructure that everything else builds on -- the SQLite database setup, Pydantic models shared across the app, the application context container, and dependency injection wiring.

tour.title: API Routers: How Requests Enter the System
tour.description: The routers define every HTTP and WebSocket endpoint. They handle incoming webhooks from external services, REST calls from the dashboard UI, and real-time event streams.

tour.title: LLM Dispatch and OpenAI Integration
tour.description: The brain of Cyborg: the LLM dispatch service orchestrates conversations, the OpenAI service handles model calls, and prompt history tracks token usage and cache performance.

tour.title: Tools and the Tool Registry
tour.description: Cyborg acts through tools. The tool registry wires every available tool into the LLM session, and individual tool modules (email, phone, memory, contacts, workspace, etc.) define what the agent can actually do.

tour.title: Session and Memory Management
tour.description: Sessions are the core abstraction for ongoing conversations. The session route service manages conversation state, the agenda service plans upcoming actions, and the memory service provides persistent recall across sessions.

tour.title: Communication Channels: WhatsApp, Email, and Voice
tour.description: Cyborg reaches people across channels. The WhatsApp bridge service connects to the Go-based bridge, email services handle polling and delivery via AgentMail, and voice services manage real-time audio conversations with TTS/STT engines.

tour.title: WhatsApp Bridge (Go Service)
tour.description: A separate Go microservice that maintains the WhatsApp Web connection. It communicates with the Python server via WebSocket, handles message queuing, and translates between WhatsApp protocol and Cyborg's internal message format.

tour.title: Dashboard UI (React/TypeScript)
tour.description: The web dashboard built with TanStack Router. It shows session history, LLM usage charts, contacts, workspace files, and provides real-time updates via WebSocket. The API client and WS hook connect it to the Python backend.

tour.title: Subagents, Skills, and Evaluation
tour.description: Advanced agent capabilities: subagent service enables delegating tasks to specialized agents, the skill developer automates creating new tools, and the eval framework provides automated quality assessment across scenarios like email handling, voice chat, and tool calling.
